d0_user_journey.action: Selects a local Git repository, an Understand-Anything graph bound to the same exact revision, and the architecture or Hackathon profile.
d0_user_journey.observable_result: The CLI reports the exact source commit, graph hash, freshness state, planned files, external actions, and duration budget.

d0_user_journey.action: Slices the graph and obtains a schema-valid, source-grounded English storyboard from the configured GPT-5.6 planning boundary.
d0_user_journey.observable_result: Every material narration segment references an allowed graph or source artifact and passes independent review.

d0_user_journey.action: Synthesizes measured English speech, compiles SRT and WebVTT captions, and renders deterministic visual scenes.
d0_user_journey.observable_result: A local MP4 with synchronized narration and captions is produced without executing repository content.

d0_user_journey.action: Checks graph/source identity, grounding, duration, subtitles, A/V streams, hashes, and governance provenance.
d0_user_journey.observable_result: A machine-readable pass or fail receipt and source-linked transcript are produced.

data_boundaries: Only graph-selected, repository-contained, allowlisted source paths may be read
Credential paths and secret-like excerpts are blocked or redacted without recording secret values
External speech receives narration text and voice settings only, never raw repository source
Private repository data combined with untrusted content and external network calls is not auto-approved
decision_to_unlock: Determine whether a graph-native compiler can reliably produce a grounded, narrated, verified code-learning video and support a subsequent learner-value pilot.

demo_beats.acceptance: The demo identifies the graph hash and source commit and blocks stale, unknown, dirty, or escaping inputs.
demo_beats.description: Strict graph and source snapshot validation

demo_beats.acceptance: Architecture and Hackathon profiles produce schema-valid scenes whose required claims retain source references through duration compression.
Measured English narration, SRT, WebVTT, optional burn-in, and deterministic visuals produce a decodable MP4 below the hard duration.
The TEMPO repository is explained by a local video whose receipt binds source, storyboard, narration, captions, media, governance, Codex, and GPT-5.6 provenance.

kill_and_pivot_triggers.condition: A bounded graph feasibility spike cannot produce a coherent grounded proof video.
Required narration cannot fit naturally below the strict 180-second limit after optional content is removed.
The implementation requires executing untrusted repository code or transmitting raw source to an external speech provider.

out_of_scope: Replacing or modifying the Understand-Anything analyzer
A nonlinear video editor or drag-and-drop timeline
Avatars, lip synchronization, background music, or generative B-roll
Automatic YouTube upload or Devpost submission
External speech calls without a valid warrant, declared provider, data boundary, and cost cap
Claims of measured learning improvement without observed user evidence

post_mvp_decision_meeting.decision_question: Should the verified vertical slice be submitted and then distributed for a multi-developer learning comparison after Build Week?

rollback_or_disposal_plan: Keep source and machine receipts, remove generated temporary frames and unauthorized external-provider outputs, and never publish the video or submit to Devpost without a separate human action.

success_metrics.measurement_method: The independent validator checks every material narration segment against existing graph/source references.
success_metrics.name: Grounded required narration

success_metrics.measurement_method: ffprobe media duration recorded by the independent verifier.
success_metrics.name: Hackathon hard duration

success_metrics.measurement_method: Caption verifier matches monotonic SRT and VTT cues to every measured speech segment.
success_metrics.name: Spoken scene subtitle coverage

success_metrics.measurement_method: Automated test inventory and complete suite receipt.
success_metrics.name: Required acceptance-test cases implemented

success_metrics.measurement_method: Timed clean-clone judge-path run after a graph is available.
success_metrics.name: Operator setup after graph generation

supported_audience: Hackathon judges testing a Developer Tool
supported_environment: Development on Windows, macOS, or Linux with Node.js 22+, pnpm 10+, Git, FFmpeg, and ffprobe
Prepared judge bundle on Windows, macOS, or Linux with Node.js 22+, Git, FFmpeg, and ffprobe; no pnpm or source rebuild
Credential-free synthetic fixture test path plus a separate real TEMPO checkout and graph dogfood path
technical_boundaries: Consume the pinned Understand-Anything graph contract; do not replace its analyzer
Model output is declarative schema-valid JSON and is never executed
The core remains independent of one speech provider and one renderer
FFmpeg-based media work is invoked through argument arrays with bounded inputs
Hackathon runs require a fresh graph, a known commit, and a clean source tree
